spawn_on_completion.description: Migration runner with dual SQLite+Pg CI support
spawn_on_completion.prompt:
MISSION: Complete PG-002 — 'Migration runner with dual SQLite+Pg CI support' — Phase 3-1.

Create a migration runner that can target either SQLite or Postgres based on env config.

## Context
- PG-001 (Phase 1.2) just completed — all .pg.sql.ts schema files exist with proper pg-core definitions
- Phase 1.4 already has drizzle.config.pg.ts configured
- Phase 0.5 installed PGlite and pg packages
- Phase 1.3 has PgAdapter in adapter.ts

## Requirements
1. Ensure drizzle-kit can generate Postgres DDL from .pg.sql.ts files
2. Run 'bun install' first in packages/opencode/ to materialize Postgres driver dependencies
3. Create migration runner that applies pg migrations on startup
4. Test suite can run against both SQLite (fast feedback) and Pg (correctness)
5. CI matrix: SQLite + Pg service container

## Files to touch
- packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts (ensure migration loading path)
- packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts (add Postgres migration loading path alongside SQLite)
- packages/opencode/package.json (verify deps)

Return a handoff JSON with lane_id='PG-002', status, files_created/modified, verification details.

spawn_on_completion.description: Extract Electron assumptions from config resolution
spawn_on_completion.prompt:
MISSION: Complete DC-001 — 'Extract Electron assumptions from config resolution' — Phase 3-2.

Study the desktop app's config resolution to find and catalog all Electron-specific assumptions.

## Context
- Phase 2 extracted ipc.ts sufficiently that PG-001 is now done
- The desktop app assumes Electron everywhere
- Config resolution assumes filesystem paths via app.getPath()

## Requirements
1. Read and audit all app.getPath calls in packages/desktop/ and packages/app/
2. Classify each as Electron-only vs portable
3. List every Electron API used in the renderer code path
4. For each: determine if it can be mocked, polyfilled, or needs extraction
5. Focus on config resolution (bootstrap, path config, workspace config)

## Return
Return a handoff JSON with lane_id='DC-001', status, an 'artifacts_created' list (esp the audit artifact), findings_summary, and blockers. Recommend the follow-on approach (what to extract first).

spawn_on_completion.description: Renderer loads in Chrome without Electron
spawn_on_completion.prompt:
MISSION: Complete DC-002 — 'Renderer loads in Chrome without Electron' — Phase 3-2.

Goal: open localhost:5173 in Chrome and see the full app UI working. No Electron APIs in the renderer code path.

## Context
- DC-001 audit (which must have completed) details every Electron API in the renderer
- Each API needs: mock, polyfill, or extract
- The renderer is SolidJS-based in packages/app/

## Requirements
1. Read the DC-001 audit findings
2. Create polyfills/mocks for each Electron API used in renderer
3. Ensure the Vite dev server (localhost:5173) starts without Electron
4. Key APIs to handle: preload bridge, ipcRenderer, dialog, shell, clipboard

## Files to touch
- packages/app/src/ (renderer code)
- packages/app/vite.config.ts (dev server config)

Return a handoff JSON with lane_id='DC-002', status, files_created/modified, verification.

spawn_on_completion.description: Plugin system decoupled from Electron IPC
spawn_on_completion.prompt:
MISSION: Complete DC-003 — 'Plugin system decoupled from Electron IPC' — Phase 3-2.

Plugins currently communicate via Electron IPC channels. Need abstract transport layer so plugins work in Chrome (postMessage) and Electron (ipcRenderer).

## Context
- DC-001 must have audited the Electron assumptions
- Plugin system lives in packages/opencode/src/plugin/
- Plugins use IPC channels in packages/desktop/src/main/

## Requirements
1. Design PluginTransport interface
2. Implement ElectronIpcTransport (existing behavior)
3. Implement PostMessageTransport (Chrome)
4. Wire transport selection at plugin load time

## Files to touch
- packages/opencode/src/plugin/
- packages/desktop/src/main/plugin/

Return a handoff JSON with lane_id='DC-003', status, files_created/modified, verification.

MISSION: Complete HC-001 — 'Break up 503-line ipc.ts god module' — Phase 3-3.

Phase 2 extracted github-ipc.ts from the main ipc.ts. The remaining 503 lines are a god module mixing MCP handlers, session management, file system ops, and window controls.

## Context
- ipc.ts is at packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts
- After github-ipc extraction, 503 lines remain
- Each concern needs its own module

## Requirements
1. Inventory ipc.ts by concern: how many distinct handler groups exist?
2. Classify each group: what module should it live in?
3. Create a refactoring plan (not the refactor itself — just the plan)
4. Identify IPC channels used, parameter types, return types
5. Check if there's overlap with DC-001/DC-003 transport work

## Return
Return a handoff JSON with lane_id='HC-001', status, artifacts_created (esp the refactoring plan), findings_summary, and a recommended extraction order.

parallel_spawn: All 5 spawn simultaneously as background:true tasks. PG-002 depends only on PG-001. DC-002, DC-003, HC-001 depend on DC-001 (which also unblocks from PG-001). Spawn all 5 immediately when PG-001 completes.
